ab: kana 2022’ami, na séhu paladuk da kayhiyan kana tu bibunsiyo na Yuéncumin karuwa putukasa puvalray da kangadanan.
ch: 在2022年，政府召開了原住民身分證可單列族名的會議。
en: In 2022, the government announced that indigenous identification cards can include tribal names separately.

ab: kiumalr ku kana vangsaran i dekalr i Lahay Ruvulreng, mau na pa’eceng lra kiami kana séhu da “putukasa da kangadanan” kananta bibunsiyo intaw.
ch: 我訪問了部落青年Lahay Ruvulreng他是長期向政府爭取我們的身分證能「單列族名」的人。
en: I interviewed Lahay Ruvulreng, a young tribal member who has long been advocating for the inclusion of tribal names on identification cards.

ab: marekuret mi kanini na kasede’an yawa ku kantaw kiumalra.
ch: 我和他約定好這個假日到他家訪問他。
en: We had arranged to visit his home this holiday for the interview.

ab: kiumaumalr ku mu, tu umalray ku kanantu kangadanan na ipakalrangan di tu adayaw ku pana’u kanantu saruma’ean di kanantu dekalr.
ch: 在訪問的過程中，他向我介紹了他的名字的由來，也帶我認識了他的家人、他的部落。
en: During the interview, he introduced to me the origin of his name and also introduced me to his family and tribe.

ab: kiumalr ku kanintaw man nu asalraw nanu ngadan, adi ’inava na maluman na puvalray diyamaw?
ch: 我問他為什麼要改名，舊的登記方式不好嗎?
en: I asked him why he insisted on changing his name. Wasn’t the old registration method sufficient?

ab: marengay kaningku, alra na tinararutiya na valray mu, adi maruwa penatedelr puvalray kanantu ngadan, di ulra na duma na cau “Lasinsen” kema mavina kanintaw, semalra malayas ki’apa’apa. kema.
ch: 他對我說，因為用中文字並不能準確的傳達他真正的名字，其他人還會叫他「拉先生」，也因此鬧出不少笑話。
en: He told me that using Chinese characters could not accurately convey his name, and others would still call him \"La Xian Sheng,\" which resulted in many jokes being made.

ab: marengay i Lahay Ruvulreng “na vinalrayan kananta valray na Yuéncumin mu, tatena’ mau nanta kangadanan. nangku ngadan pinucepa lra kananiyam mauwan, kananiyam pa’atayan, kananiyam inucauwan, ’inava nu kaduwan na cau tu acuvungaw nantu kangadanan. kema.
ch: Lahay Ruvulreng說：「原住民語書寫的名字才是我們真正的名字。我的名字代表了我們的族群、我們的家族、我們的身分，希望能有更多族人找回自己的名字。」
en: Lahay Ruvulreng said, \"The names written in indigenous languages are our true names. My name represents our ethnic group, our family, our identity. I hope more fellow tribespeople can reclaim their own names.\"
